exemplar.title: The relationship between the resistance of a wire and the length of a wire

detailedExemplarData.title: The relationship between the resistance of a wire and the length of a wire

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Raw and processed data tables are neatly labelled with units and uncertainties, and symbols are defined.
Uncertainties (absolute and percentage) are calculated and propagated, with error bars plotted on the graph.
Data processing (averages, resistances, linear regression, gradient comparison) is carried out accurately and presented with clarity and precision.
weaknesses: A few propagation formulae are mis-typed and duplicate headings in tables suggest minor organizational oversights.
One sample uncertainty propagation uses an additive method instead of root-sum-squares.
Some variables in the gradient calculation (e.g., parameter G) are introduced without definition.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data recording and processing is both clear and precise (band 5â6), and data processing is carried out appropriately and accurately (band 5â6). Consideration of uncertainties has a few omissions (band 3â4). On balance, the work demonstrates largely precise communication and accurate processing, warranting 5 marks.

strengths: Research question is placed in a specific, real-world household wiring context with appropriate citations.
The theoretical basis (R=ÏL/A) is clearly introduced and linked to the experimental variables.
A detailed equipment list and stepwise procedure (including length ranges, intervals, and trial counts) largely allow reproducibility.
weaknesses: Exact supply voltage and wire tension setup are omitted, leaving minor ambiguities in the procedure.
Justification for 0.1 m length increments and temperature control is brief and would benefit from further explanation.
Some methodological considerations (e.g., rationale for current range) are described rather than fully explained.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The context and research question presentation align with the top band (5â6), but the methodology description has a few ambiguities (missing supply voltage, tension details) and methodological considerations are described rather than fully explained. Overall this places the criterion solidly in the 3â4 band, meriting 4 marks.

strengths: The conclusion correctly restates the directly proportional relationship and links it to the linear graph.
Experimental and theoretical gradients are compared, and error magnitudes are discussed.
Distinction between small random error bars and systematic error is noted, providing context.
weaknesses: The degree of agreement between experimental and theoretical values is not quantified numerically.
Sources of systematic error (contact resistance, heating effects) are mentioned only generally and lack specific discussion.
The scientific context comparison is relevant but not fully developed to justify conclusions at a deeper level.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is consistent with the analysis and provides some context but lacks full quantitative justification and depth in scientific comparison. This performance lies in the 3â4 band; a score of 3 reflects the need for more detailed justification and deeper contextual linkage.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (ruler eye-reading, multimeter fluctuations, limited range) are identified.
A realistic improvement (using an ohmmeter) is proposed with brief justification.
weaknesses: The impact of identified limitations is not quantified or prioritized.
Only one improvement is described; further realistic suggestions (e.g., four-wire measurement, temperature control, thicker wire) are absent.
Improvements are stated rather than described or explained in detail.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student describes specific weaknesses (band 3â4), suggested improvements are limited to a single realistic action and are not fully described. This aligns with the 1â2 band, meriting 2 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student situates the research question in a clearly defined, authentic context: everyday household wiring, supported by cited sources and a concise theoretical explanation of resistivity.  The wording of the question itself is precise (nichrome wire, resistance vs. length), and the background section explicitly connects the investigation to the governing equation R = ÏL/A.  This specificity exceeds a broad outline and therefore aligns with the top-band description; minor stylistic lapses do not detract from the appropriateness of the context.

Instrument uncertainties are stated, absolute and percentage uncertainties are calculated for V, I, R, and the gradient; error bars are plotted.  Nonetheless, some propagation formulae are mis-typed, and duplicate headings suggest organisational omissions.  Overall consideration is sound but not consistently rigorous, aligning with âevidence â¦ with some significant omissionsâ.

The student identifies relevant considerationsâconstant wire resistivity, cross-sectional area, temperature, instrument choice, trial numberâand justifies some of them (e.g., same multimeters to reduce system error).  However, explanations remain brief; there is little discussion of why eight increments or why 0.1 m steps ensure sufficiency, and no rationale for the chosen current range.  This constitutes âdescribedâ rather than fully âexplainedâ methodological considerations.

Specific weaknesses are identified: eye-reading of ruler, fluctuating multimeter readings, limited length range, and an assumption of proportionality.  The student links these to possible random or systematic errors but stops short of quantitatively estimating their impact.  That corresponds to âdescribes specific weaknessesâ (3-4 band).

Equipment lists, step-by-step procedure, ranges (0.1â0.8 m), intervals, and three trials are provided, allowing another student to reproduce the work with only a few ambiguities (e.g., exact supply voltage, wire tension, method for zeroing meters).  The overall description is detailed but not exhaustive; therefore it matches the 3-4 band rather than the fully comprehensive 5-6 band.

Averages, resistance values (V/I), propagated uncertainties, linear regression, and comparison with theoretical gradient are carried out correctly; numerical results are sensible and internally consistent.  Apart from rounding differences, there are no major inaccuracies, satisfying the descriptor for appropriate and accurate processing.

One realistic improvement (use an ohmmeter) is proposed and briefly justified.  No additional concrete actions (e.g., four-wire measurement, temperature monitoring, thicker wire to reduce heating) are discussed.  Therefore the strand demonstrates a stated but not fully described set of improvements, aligning with the 1-2 mark band.

Raw-data tables are neatly labelled with units and uncertainties; processed tables show averages, propagated uncertainties, and symbols are defined.  The graph includes axes labels, units, error bars, best-fit line and gradient boxes.  Minor typographical slips (duplicate column heading, capital âIâ mis-label) are negligible, so the communication is judged both clear and precise.

The student compares experimental gradient to the theoretical Ï/A and discusses the large percentage difference, attributing it to systematic error.  This constitutes a relevant comparison, but the explanation lacks depth (no discussion of contact resistance, heating, or calibration).  Hence the strand fits the mid-band rather than the top band.

The conclusion restates the directly proportional relationship and cites the linear graph and equation.  It also comments on the magnitude of systematic error and the meaning of small error bars.  While generally consistent with the analysis, the justification is brief and does not fully explore quantitative agreement; thus it fits the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively situates the investigation in a household context and cites relevant sources, enhancing the research context.
The research question is stated precisely and linked to nichrome wire properties, providing clarity for the investigation.
The formula $$R=\rho\frac{L}{A}$$ is correctly introduced and explained, linking theory to the experimental variables.
The description of length measurement lacks justification for 0.1 m increments ensuring data sufficiency; explain why this provides adequate resolution.
Conducting three trials per length increment demonstrates reproducibility and reduces random error in data collection.
The controlled variables are stated, but the justification for maintaining constant temperature is brief; explain how temperature was monitored.
The equipment list is comprehensive but omits the exact supply voltage and wire tension setup; include these details for full reproducibility.
The procedural steps are clearly enumerated and mirror the diagram, facilitating reproducibility of the experimental setup.
Safety measures are described and appropriate for electrical experiments, reducing risk; consider adding wire heating precautions.
The environmental/ethical section notes no issues but could discuss safe disposal or handling of heated wire to complete the evaluation.
The raw-data table includes uncertainties for length, voltage, and current, presented with clear headings and units.
The symbol list repeats â\overline{Îl}â twice, indicating an organizational oversight; remove the duplicate and clarify definitions.
The description of percentage uncertainty in the symbol list appears mis-typed as ââ[6]{2}â; correct the formula to match the propagated uncertainty.
Sample uncertainty calculations are shown stepwise, which enhances transparency and demonstrates proper uncertainty handling.
Table headers in the processed data table are duplicated (ÎI appears twice), which could confuse interpretation; adjust heading labels.
Resistance values and their uncertainties are calculated accurately and presented clearly in Table 3.
The sample calculation for propagation of resistance uncertainty incorrectly uses additive roots; revise the propagation method to root-sum-of-squares.
The graph includes error bars, axes labels, and a best-fit line, presenting data processing with clarity and precision.
Table 4 presents resistance vs. length clearly but omits uncertainty values; include propagated uncertainties to complete analysis.
Theoretical and experimental gradients are correctly calculated and compared, reinforcing the link between data and scientific context.
The comparison of gradient difference mentions systematic and random errors but lacks specific discussion of sources such as contact resistance or heating.
Uncertainty in the gradient is calculated but the parameter G is introduced without definition; define variables before use for clarity.
The conclusion restates the directly proportional relationship and aligns with the linear graph through the origin.
The conclusion mentions agreement with theory but does not quantify the degree of agreement; include numerical justification.
The differentiation of precision (small error bars) versus systematic error is insightful and effectively contextualizes the results.
Methodological weaknesses (ruler reading, multimeter fluctuations) are described with specificity, linking to error sources.
The impact of identified limitations is described but not quantified; consider estimating how these errors affect measured gradients.
Using an ohmmeter is a realistic improvement to measure resistance directly, enhancing precision.
Specify the supply voltage and current range in the equipment section to eliminate ambiguity and improve reproducibility.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â

similarExemplars.title:
What is the effect of increasing the surface area of a nichrome wire by multiplying the number of
wires by a multiple of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10 wires, if the length of the wire, Temperature,
the multimeter, and purity of nichrome is constant.

the multimeter, and purity of nichrome is constant.
